title: TessellateFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a tessellate feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: aspectRatio
properties.description: Specify ratio between the height and width of each face on the mesh body. Only valid if tessellateRefinementType is CustomTessellateRefinementType.

properties.name: createQuads
properties.description: Creates quad faces on the mesh body where possible.

properties.name: inputBodies
properties.description: Gets and sets the input list of BReb bodies.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: maximumEdgeLength
properties.description: Specify maximum length of any face edge on the mesh body. Only valid if tessellateRefinementType is CustomTessellateRefinementType.

properties.name: normalDeviation
properties.description: Specify maximum angle between the normal vectors of each face on the mesh body. Only valid if tessellateRefinementType is CustomTessellateRefinementType.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: surfaceDeviation
properties.description: Specify maximum distance between the surface of the original body and the surface of the mesh body. Only valid if tessellateRefinementType is CustomTessellateRefinementType.

properties.name: targetBaseFeature
properties.description: When creating a feature that is owned by a base feature, set this property to the base feature you want to associate the new feature with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a feature associated with a base feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

properties.name: tessellateRefinementType
properties.description: Gets and sets the type of refinement, default value is MediumTessellateRefinementType.

aspectRatio.title: TessellateFeatureInput.aspectRatio Property
aspectRatio.description: Specify ratio between the height and width of each face on the mesh body. Only valid if tessellateRefinementType is CustomTessellateRefinementType.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = tessellateFeatureInput_var.aspectRatio

# Set the value of the property.
tessellateFeatureInput_var.aspectRatio = propertyValue
